inputs.query: We're a mid-size US automaker. Analyze the EV competitive landscape in Europe and recommend a market-entry strategy for 2027.

outputs.answer: Recommended 2027 EU market-entry strategy: enter via Germany and the Netherlands first (highest EV adoption + charging density), position in the mid-price C-segment to undercut VW ID.3/ID.4 by ~8%, partner with Ionity for charging, and localize battery assembly in Hungary to qualify for EU content incentives. Phase into France/Nordics in H2-2027 once brand awareness is established.

messages.content: We're a mid-size US automaker. Analyze the EV competitive landscape in Europe and recommend a market-entry strategy for 2027.

outputs.content: Plan: 1) Research EU EV market 2) Analyze competition & sizing 3) Write strategy report. Route first to Research Agent.

inputs.task: Gather EV market data for Europe (demand, players, charging infra).

outputs.summary: EU EV market is large and fast-growing; DE=volume, NO/NL=penetration; charging infra strong in DE/NL. Sources validated.

inputs.query: Europe EV sales 2025 2026 by country market share

results: EU BEV share of new cars ~16% in 2025; Norway >80%.
Top OEMs: Tesla, VW Group, Stellantis, BMW, Hyundai-Kia.
Germany largest volume market; Norway/NL highest penetration.

messages.content: Is the research sufficient to analyze?

outputs.content: Research solid. Route to Analysis Agent.

inputs.task: Size the market and benchmark competitors.

outputs.finding: Room in mid-price C-segment (~€38k) with charging-network partnership; MG owns bottom, VW/Tesla own premium-mid.

outputs.gap: mid-price with strong charging partnership underserved

messages.content: Is the analysis complete enough to write?

outputs.content: Gap: analysis ignores EU regulatory/subsidy landscape (CO2 targets, local-content incentives). Loop back to Research Agent for a regulatory addendum before writing.

inputs.task: Addendum: EU EV regulation, CO2 targets & local-content subsidy rules for 2027.

outputs.summary: Regulatory tailwind: tightening CO2 targets favor BEVs; local-content rules reward EU battery assembly (Hungary).

inputs.query: EU 2027 CO2 fleet targets EV local content subsidy rules

results: EU CO2 fleet target tightens 2025-2030; pushes OEM BEV mix.
Local battery content increasingly tied to purchase incentives.

messages.content: Do we now have enough to write?

outputs.content: Complete. Route to Report Writer Agent.

inputs.task: Draft the market-entry strategy report from research + analysis + regulatory addendum.

outputs.report: Recommended 2027 EU market-entry strategy: enter via Germany and the Netherlands first (highest EV adoption + charging density), position in the mid-price C-segment to undercut VW ID.3/ID.4 by ~8%, partner with Ionity for charging, and localize battery assembly in Hungary to qualify for EU content incentives. Phase into France/Nordics in H2-2027 once brand awareness is established.

messages.content: Synthesize findings into a 2027 EU entry plan.

outputs.content: Recommended 2027 EU market-entry strategy: enter via Germany and the Netherlands first (highest EV adoption + charging density), position in the mid-price C-segment to undercut VW ID.3/ID.4 by ~8%, partner with Ionity for charging, and localize battery assembly in Hungary to qualify for EU content incentives. Phase into France/Nordics in H2-2027 once brand awareness is established.

outputs.report: Recommended 2027 EU market-entry strategy: enter via Germany and the Netherlands first (highest EV adoption + charging density), position in the mid-price C-segment to undercut VW ID.3/ID.4 by ~8%, partner with Ionity for charging, and localize battery assembly in Hungary to qualify for EU content incentives. Phase into France/Nordics in H2-2027 once brand awareness is established.

outputs.content: Approved. Report answers the query with data + regulatory context.
